chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Acq. Appeal No. S- 87 of 2015 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 25.01.2016 1. For Orders on Office Objection 2. For Katcha Peshi 3. For Orders on MA 9716/15 Ms. Nayyar Sultana Shaikh, Advocate for Appellant Through this Criminal Acquittal Appeal, the appellant has challenged the judgment dated 25.8.2015 passed in Crime No. 25 of 2013 registered at police station Tando Ghulam Hyder under Section 395, 341, 147, 148 and 149 PPC, whereby the respondents have been acquitted. In compliance of the order dated 7.1.2016 learned counsel has filed a copy of deposition of complainant. A perusal of the same shows that the complainant has not recognized the accused in the court and in his cross examination has admitted that the contents of FIR were not read over to him. Learned counsel appearing on behalf of appellant argues that in fact the counsel for the complainant had misguided him to give such evidence. Be that as it may, it can be hardly a ground for maintaining the acquittal appeal against the respondents. The appellant may have a cause of action against his counsel who, according to him, misguided him but once in his evidence he has taken a stance, he cannot be allowed under the law to back out from or alter it. This Cr. Acq. Appeal is dismissed in limine. JUDGE *Karar/-
